title: rosa canina var. corymbifera
intro: Rosa canina var. corymbifera, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the family Rosaceae. It is a subspecies of the Rosa canina species and is characterized by its fragrant flowers, thorny stems, and red to pink fruits. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina var. corymbifera are attributed to its high content of vitamins C and E, flavonoids, and other antioxidants, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include soothing digestive issues, reducing anxiety and stress, and promoting skin and eye health, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves for various medicinal applications.
